title: A Homestay With a Local Family

all.en: During my exchange program in Japan，I stayed with a kind host family in Kyoto.
all.zh: 在我去日本交換期間，我住在京都一戶親切的寄宿家庭中。

all.en: The Tanaka family welcomed me with smiles and a traditional homemade dinner.
all.zh: 田中家以微笑和傳統的家常晚餐歡迎我。

all.en: At first，I felt nervous because I could only speak basic Japanese.
all.zh: 一開始，我感到很緊張，因為我只會講基本的日語。

all.en: However，the family was patient and helped me practice the language every day.
all.zh: 然而，這家人很有耐心，每天都幫我練習日語。

all.en: The mother taught me how to make miso soup and rolled sushi from scratch.
all.zh: 媽媽教我如何從頭做味噌湯和卷壽司。

all.en: I shared a small but comfortable bedroom with a futon on tatami mats.
all.zh: 我住的房間雖小卻很舒適，鋪在榻榻米上的是日式被褥。

all.en: On weekends，we visited beautiful temples and walked through peaceful bamboo forests.
all.zh: 週末時，我們造訪美麗的寺廟，並走過寧靜的竹林。

all.en: The youngest daughter became my best friend and showed me her favorite hidden cafes.
all.zh: 最小的女兒成了我最好的朋友，她帶我去她最喜歡的祕境咖啡店。

all.en: I learned not only the language but also the values of respect and politeness.
all.zh: 我學到的不只是語言，還有尊重和禮貌的價值觀。

all.en: When it was time to leave，we all cried and promised to stay in touch forever.
all.zh: 離別的時候，我們都哭了，並承諾要永遠保持聯絡。

all.en: That homestay experience changed my view of the world and made me feel like part of another culture.
all.zh: 那次寄宿經驗改變了我對世界的看法，也讓我覺得自己融入了另一種文化。

all.en: Back in Taiwan, I video chatted with the Tanaka family every Sunday evening.
all.zh: 回到臺灣後，我每個星期天晚上都會和田中家視訊聊天。

all.en: The youngest daughter sent me handwritten letters describing her school life in Kyoto.
all.zh: 最小的女兒寄手寫的信給我，描述她在京都的校園生活。

all.en: I practiced my Japanese every day so I could reply to her letters fluently.
all.zh: 我每天練習日文，這樣才能流利地回覆她的信。

all.en: Two years later, the family came to visit me during their summer holiday.
all.zh: 兩年後，這家人趁暑假來臺灣拜訪我。

all.en: I took them to the night markets, where they tried bubble tea for the very first time.
all.zh: 我帶他們去夜市，他們生平第一次嚐到了珍珠奶茶。

all.en: The mother loved Taiwanese beef noodle soup so much that she asked for the recipe.
all.zh: 那位媽媽很喜歡臺灣牛肉麵，還向我要食譜。

all.en: My own mother and the Tanaka mother bonded instantly over their love of cooking.
all.zh: 我媽媽和田中媽媽因為對烹飪的共同熱愛，立刻就熟絡起來。

all.en: We promised to celebrate every important holiday together, no matter the distance.
all.zh: 我們約定不論距離多遠，每個重要節日都要一起慶祝。

all.en: My host family taught me that home is not a place but the people who love you.
all.zh: 我的寄宿家庭讓我明白，家不是一個地方，而是愛你的那些人。

all.en: Their kindness continues to influence the way I treat strangers and friends today.
all.zh: 他們的善意至今仍影響著我對待陌生人和朋友的方式。

all.en: Three years later, I traveled back to Kyoto to celebrate the youngest daughter's graduation.
all.zh: 三年後，我回到京都慶祝最小女兒的畢業典禮。

all.en: The Tanaka family met me at the train station with a bouquet of pink cherry blossoms.
all.zh: 田中家在火車站以一束粉色櫻花迎接我。

all.en: My Japanese had improved so much that we held entire conversations without difficulty.
all.zh: 我的日語進步神速，我們能夠毫無困難地進行整段對話。

all.en: We attended the graduation ceremony together and cheered loudly when her name was called.
all.zh: 我們一起參加畢業典禮，當她的名字被叫到時，我們大聲歡呼。

all.en: That evening, the mother prepared all of my favorite traditional dishes from my exchange days.
all.zh: 那天晚上，媽媽準備了我交換時期最愛的所有傳統菜餚。

all.en: I brought them Taiwanese pineapple cakes and oolong tea as small thank-you gifts.
all.zh: 我帶了臺灣的鳳梨酥和烏龍茶作為小小的謝禮。

all.en: The youngest daughter announced she wanted to study abroad in Taiwan next year.
all.zh: 最小的女兒宣布她明年想到臺灣留學。

all.en: My family immediately offered to host her, returning the kindness we had once received.
all.zh: 我的家人立刻表示願意接待她，回報我們曾經受到的關照。

all.en: We spent our last night together watching old photographs and laughing until midnight.
all.zh: 我們最後一晚一起翻看舊照片，一直笑到午夜。

all.en: Our bond had grown stronger over time, proving that true family knows no borders.
all.zh: 我們的情感隨著時間越發深厚，證明真正的家人沒有國界之分。

all.en: The youngest Tanaka daughter arrived in Taipei the following autumn for her exchange program.
all.zh: 田中家最小的女兒在隔年秋天抵達臺北參加她的交換計畫。

all.en: My family welcomed her with a homemade Taiwanese feast and traditional festival decorations.
all.zh: 我的家人以家常臺灣盛宴和傳統節慶裝飾迎接她。

all.en: She quickly fell in love with night markets and tried every bubble tea shop near our home.
all.zh: 她很快愛上了夜市，並嚐遍了我們家附近的每一間珍珠奶茶店。

all.en: My mother taught her how to make beef noodle soup using our family's secret broth recipe.
all.zh: 我媽媽用我們家祕傳的湯頭食譜教她做牛肉麵。

all.en: On weekends, I took her hiking on the trails that surround the Taipei mountains.
all.zh: 週末時，我帶她去走環繞臺北山區的健行步道。

all.en: Her Mandarin improved rapidly, and she made friends easily at her language school.
all.zh: 她的中文進步快速，在語言學校也輕鬆地交到了朋友。

all.en: We celebrated Lunar New Year together, and she helped my family clean and decorate our home.
all.zh: 我們一起慶祝農曆新年，她幫我家人打掃並布置家裡。

all.en: My grandparents adored her gentle manners and her sincere effort to learn our customs.
all.zh: 我的祖父母很喜愛她溫和的舉止和她真誠學習我們習俗的努力。

all.en: When her year ended, she promised to invite my entire family to Kyoto next spring.
all.zh: 當她的交換結束時，她承諾明年春天要邀請我們全家去京都。

all.en: Our two families had now become one extended family across two beautiful countries.
all.zh: 我們兩個家庭如今已成為橫跨兩個美麗國度的一個大家庭。

all.en: During the Tanaka daughter's stay in Taipei, our two families video chatted every weekend.
all.zh: 田中家女兒在臺北期間，我們兩家每個週末都會視訊聊天。

all.en: The Tanaka mother began learning Mandarin so she could communicate more with my parents.
all.zh: 田中媽媽開始學習中文，以便能與我父母有更多交流。

all.en: My own mother, in turn, started taking Japanese classes once a week at a community center.
all.zh: 我媽媽也回報式地開始每週在社區中心上一次日語課。

all.en: The following spring, both families finally met in Kyoto under the full cherry blossoms.
all.zh: 隔年春天，兩家人終於在櫻花盛開的京都相聚。

all.en: We held a small ceremony where the Tanaka grandmother adopted my parents as honorary siblings.
all.zh: 我們舉行了一場小型儀式，田中奶奶把我父母收為榮譽兄妹。

all.en: My father and Mr. Tanaka discovered a shared love for fishing during a quiet morning trip.
all.zh: 我爸爸和田中先生在一個寧靜的早晨垂釣行中發現了對釣魚的共同熱愛。

all.en: They promised to take an annual fishing trip together for as long as their health allowed.
all.zh: 他們約定只要身體允許，每年都要一起去釣魚。

all.en: The youngest Tanaka daughter began a Taiwan-Japan cultural blog with thousands of followers.
all.zh: 田中家最小的女兒開始經營一個臺日文化部落格，擁有數千位粉絲。

all.en: My family planned to visit Kyoto every year, just as she planned to visit us in Taipei.
all.zh: 我家計畫每年都去京都，就像她計畫每年都來臺北看我們一樣。

all.en: Our extended family across two countries felt richer than I had ever dared to imagine.
all.zh: 我們橫跨兩國的大家庭，比我曾敢想像的更加豐盛。

all.en: Every spring, our two families now alternated hosting cherry blossom and lantern festival celebrations.
all.zh: 如今每年春天，我們兩家輪流舉辦櫻花季與燈籠祭的慶祝活動。

all.en: My mother and Mrs. Tanaka began co-writing a bilingual cookbook of family festival recipes.
all.zh: 我母親與田中太太開始合作撰寫一本雙語的家族節慶食譜。

all.en: The book was small but lovingly produced, with handwritten notes and watercolor illustrations.
all.zh: 這本書雖小卻製作精心，配有手寫筆記與水彩插畫。

all.en: Both families sold the book at local markets, donating proceeds to youth cultural exchange programs.
all.zh: 兩家在當地市集販售這本書，將收益捐給青年文化交流計畫。

all.en: The youngest Tanaka daughter eventually studied translation at a Taipei university for two full years.
all.zh: 田中家最小的女兒後來在臺北大學讀了整整兩年的翻譯系。

all.en: She lived in our spare bedroom and called my parents Mama and Papa with great affection.
all.zh: 她住在我們的客房，並以極深的情感稱呼我父母為媽媽和爸爸。

all.en: My younger brother began studying Japanese seriously and won a small national speech contest.
all.zh: 我弟弟開始認真學習日文，並贏得了一個小型的全國演講比賽。

all.en: He delivered his winning speech in both languages, dedicating it to our two-country family bond.
all.zh: 他用兩種語言發表了得獎演講，將這份榮譽獻給我們跨兩國的家庭情誼。

all.en: Mr. Tanaka attended the contest and quietly cried during my brother's gentle closing sentence.
all.zh: 田中先生出席了比賽，並在我弟弟溫柔的結語中靜靜流下淚水。

all.en: One small homestay summer had truly woven two families into one warm, lifelong embrace.
all.zh: 一個小小的暑期寄宿，真正將兩個家庭編織成一個溫暖、終生的擁抱。
